steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes the beads' presence across many MSA sites in western North Africa. The passage attributes this pattern to two causes: the evolution of symbolic behavior (motivation to make ornaments) and widespread shell availability (raw material). The word must describe what those two causes together produce.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘expediency’: means suitability or convenience. The passage is not saying the beads were practical; it is explaining why they appear at many sites. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘fecundity’: means fertility or productive abundance. The passage is not about the shells reproducing or the beads producing more beads. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘ubiquity’: means widespread presence. Symbolic behavior evolution and shell availability across the region naturally explain the beads' presence at many sites. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘heterogeneity’: means diversity or variation. The passage emphasizes that similar shell beads have been found across many sites, not that the beads vary widely. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
